content.text:
Now I have a clear 3D view of the molecule with all atom indices labeled. Looking specifically at atom C:9 (carbon with index 9), I can clearly identify its neighbor atoms by examining the bonds connected to it:

From the image, atom C:9 is bonded to:
1. H:10 (hydrogen, index 10) - white sphere directly connected to C:9
2. H:11 (hydrogen, index 11) - white sphere directly connected to C:9  
3. C:7 (carbon, index 7) - yellow/orange sphere directly connected to C:9
4. S:12 (sulfur, index 12) - larger yellow sphere directly connected to C:9

So atom C:9 has 4 neighbor atoms: H:10, H:11, C:7, and S:12.

The geomconf_index is 7582, as indicated by the filename "geomconf_7582.xyz".

Let me confirm this is correct by checking if all these connections are covalent bonds (which they appear to be based on the molecular structure visualization).

The neighbor atoms of atom C:9 are:
- Atom index 10, element H
- Atom index 11, element H  
- Atom index 7, element C
- Atom index 12, element S

Geomconf_index: 7582

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 9, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
